title: acmella lundellii
intro: Acmella lundellii, also known as the "parasol plant" or "toothache plant," is a tropical plant species native to the Americas, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The medicinal properties of Acmella lundellii lie in its unique compounds, including acmellin and spilanthol, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include relief from toothache pain, reduction of inflammation, and protection against bacterial infections. Acmella lundellii can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
